ForbiddenAbstained: Do not blame Jamie for losing the original form.
Do not say Maya is already approved for the trip.
Do not extend the April 2 deadline.
Do not reduce or waive the $12 fee.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Jamie,

I went through Maya's folder, the payment envelope, and my teacher log on March 28 for the April 9 science museum field trip (FieldTrip-4A-09). I don't have her signed permission slip or the $12 payment yet.

The front office has spare blank forms, so I can send one home today. I need both the slip and $12 by April 2—after that I can't add her to the attendance list.

I know it's frustrating since you remember signing the form, but I need the record complete before the trip. Please send a new signed slip with the $12, and I'll add her once they arrive.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not say the damage was the carrier's fault.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Lena,

I saw the photo of the cracked mug from order R4821. The replacement was delivered May 6, and the saucer looks okay.

I can send one free replacement mug once I confirm your address. A full refund isn't possible—the window closed April 30.

Please send your current delivery address by Friday at 3 p.m. If it's changed, I'll need the new one in writing before I ship.

Best,

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not say the account was canceled.
Do not change the $31.50 credit amount.
rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Marco,

I checked invoice INV-8842 for the Pro Workspace plan—issued May 2 for $186.00, covering May 1 to May 31. Your team removed three seats on May 14.

Because the seats changed mid-cycle, a $31.50 credit will show on your next invoice. A full $186.00 refund isn't possible since the workspace stayed active and the plan wasn't canceled during the refund window.

To have the $31.50 credit applied to the June invoice, please confirm by May 29. Sorry the earlier note was unclear—I don't want to promise a refund the billing policy doesn't allow.

Best,
